mission.title: Install Keylime Attestation Operator on Kubernetes
mission.description: Keylime is a TPM-based remote boot attestation and runtime integrity measurement system. The Keylime Attestation Operator deploys Keylime components (registrar, verifier, and agent) on Kubernetes to provide hardware-rooted trust and continuous node integrity monitoring using TPM 2.0 devices.

steps.title: Install the Keylime operator
steps.description:
Deploy the Keylime operator for Kubernetes:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/keylime/attestation-operator/releases/download/v0.3.0/install.yaml
```

steps.title: Deploy Keylime components
steps.description:
Create a Keylime attestation instance:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: attestation.keylime.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Keylime
metadata:
  name: keylime
  namespace: keylime-system
spec:
  verifier:
    replicas: 1
  registrar:
    replicas: 1
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keylime-system
kubectl get keylime -n keylime-system
```

steps.title: Install keylime agent on nodes (optional)
steps.description:
For host-level attestation, install the Keylime agent:
```bash
pip install keylime
keylime_agent --help
```

resolution.summary: Keylime attestation operator is installed for TPM-based remote attestation on Kubernetes.

uninstall.title: Remove Keylime
uninstall.description:
```bash
kubectl delete keylime --all -n keylime-system
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/keylime/attestation-operator/releases/download/v0.3.0/install.yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: TPM not available
troubleshooting.description:
Keylime requires TPM 2.0 on nodes for attestation:
```bash
ls /dev/tpm*
```

troubleshooting.title: Agent registration fails
troubleshooting.description:
Check the registrar logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=keylime-registrar -n keylime-system
```

prerequisites.description: Kubernetes 1.24+ cluster with worker nodes that have TPM 2.0 hardware. git, make, helm, and kubectl must be available on your workstation. For unprivileged agent pods, Kubernetes 1.26+ and the k8s-tpm-device-plugin are recommended.
